mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by CPIAUCSL above 2.5% YoY, signal central bank tightening pressures that depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions that drive positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor markets that push corporate profit margins higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE declines by >0.5pp over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) below 95.5 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1% in 50%+ of instances when INDPRO is below 95.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) creates conflicting demand signals that generate negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through market uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when RSAFS rises and HOUST falls.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k concurrent with high unemployment (UNRATE > 7%) signals labor market slack that suppresses wage inflation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances when PAYEMS exceeds 150k and UNRATE > 7%.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish shift in monetary policy, indicated by falling FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL, creates a 'higher for longer' rate environment that depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls < 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns exceed 1.5% in 50%+ of instances when FEDFUNDS declines and CPIAUCSL rises.
